name: Galaktoboureko
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy-bottomed, 3-quart saucepan, gradually bring the milk to a boil without scorching.
2. Sift the semolina, cornstarch, 1 cup of sugar, and salt together. Gradually add to the boiling milk, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
3. Cook slowly over medium heat until thickened and comes to a full boil, then remove from heat.
4. Beat eggs on high speed in an electric mixer. Gradually add ½ cup sugar, continuing to beat until very thick and fluffy (about 10 minutes).
5. Add vanilla extract to the beaten eggs.
6. Butter a 9 x 12 x 3-inch baking pan and cover the bottom with 7 sheets of filo, brushing generously with butter between each sheet.
7. Pour custard into the pan over the filo.
8. Cover with 5 remaining sheets of filo, brushing butter between each sheet and on top.
9. Score top filo into square or diamond shapes with a very sharp knife, being careful not to score as deeply as the custard.
10. Bake in a moderate (350°F) oven for 40-45 minutes until crisp and golden chestnut in color and custard is firm.
11. Meanwhile, boil remaining 1½ cups sugar with water and lemon or orange peel for 5 minutes. Add brandy or cognac if desired, then set aside.
12. Remove galaktoboureko from oven and place on a cake rack. Drizzle hot syrup over the entire galaktoboureko, especially edges.
13. Allow to cool thoroughly before cutting and serving. Store in refrigerator.
